Telok Ayer Marine & Engineering Limited (SGX-ST Mainboard-listed) detected on 18 July 2026 an unauthorised access to an external virtual data room (operated by BlueKite Data Rooms Pte. Ltd.) used for the ongoing proposed acquisition of HarbourNova Robotics Pte. Ltd.
Preliminary review confirms access to a draft share purchase agreement, board papers on the proposed acquisition, an internal unaudited H1 FY2026 trading update, and preliminary documentation for a planned dual-tranche SGD note issuance under our MTN Programme.
The H1 trading update and financing timetable are not generally available information and may be expected to materially affect the price of the Company’s securities within the meaning of Rule 703; the incident occurred within the Company’s blackout window (15 July–30 July) ahead of the H1 results release.
Personal data in the data room included names, work emails and mobile numbers of approximately 230 employees and advisors; no NRIC or passport numbers were stored, consistent with PDPA minimisation guidelines.
For clarity on public status, certain high-level FY2027 margin targets were already referred to in the Investor Day deck posted on SGXNET on 5 May 2026, but the detailed monthly run-rate figures in the trading update have not been disclosed and remain non-public.
This incident is unrelated to the network latency event disclosed in our SGXNET announcement dated 3 March 2026 and to the cost optimisation programme communicated by press release on 12 January 2026.
Immediate actions: access to the affected workspace has been suspended; credentials rotated; external forensics engaged; Mr. Adrian Seah (CISO) and Ms. Clara Toh (Group General Counsel) are coordinating containment and evidence preservation.
We have commenced assessment under the PDPA and will notify the PDPC and affected individuals as soon as practicable and no later than three calendar days if the breach is notifiable; no payment card data or health data were involved.
We are consulting SGX RegCo on whether an immediate announcement via SGXNET is required and whether a trading halt should be requested to avoid a disorderly market; no selective briefings to analysts or investors will be made.
Current yard operations and customer deliveries are unaffected; the Port of Pasir Putih’s planned crane maintenance outage on 19–21 July is a third-party operational matter and has no bearing on this incident.
Preliminary indication is that the intrusion vector was a compromised advisor account with weak MFA enrolment at the vendor; BlueKite Data Rooms Pte. Ltd. has confirmed logs retention and is preparing an incident timeline.
Subject to legal advice, we intend to remediate by migrating sensitive M&A materials to an on‑premise repository and delaying upload of the draft offering circular until after public release of H1 results.
We will review closed‑period access controls for external advisors and tighten monitoring to reduce the risk of selective disclosure during blackout windows.
Queries may be directed to dpo@tamel.sg (Data Protection Officer) or ir@tamel.sg (Investor Relations); media should contact communications@tamel.sg and refrain from speculative reporting pending any SGXNET disclosure.
